global:
We want to pivot the org-next app from using the auth app to instead use cognito directly. This means a PKCE flow complete with login, logout, and refresh capabilities. Come up with a step by step plan to implement cognito in a similar way to how the auth app is doing it but completely self-contained in the org-next app. 

Ruthlessley grill me asking questions along the way so that we are all completely confident that we are aligned on each step and each decision made in this plan.

We need to complete the connection between the org-next app and the auth app. This may require some work on either side. I'll add a picture of roughly what I'm thinking for how this would work but feel free to correct me. What I want in the org-next app is to get the session server cookies for access token, refresh token, and id token. Explain any manual tasks needed to get  this working locally. There may also be a missing backend work that needs to be done. 

Ask me questions until we are completely aligned on the plan.

Explain what this code will do
```
declare -A links=(
  ["aliases"]="$HOME/.aliases"
  ["config/cursor"]="$HOME/.cursor"
  ["config/claude"]="$HOME/.claude"
  ["config/codex"]="$HOME/.codex"
)

for src in "${!links[@]}"; do
  dest="${links[$src]}"

  echo "Backing up $dest"
  mv "$dest" "$olddir/" 2>/dev/null

  echo "Removing existing $dest"
  rm -rf "$dest"

  echo "Linking $dest -> $dir/$src"
  ln -sfn "$dir/$src" "$dest"
done
```

Here are the directories and all of the ones I want in the associative array: 
```
dir=~/git/dotfiles/public        # dotfiles directory
olddir=~/dotfiles_old             # old dotfiles backup directory

# Each of these files gets a symlink from home, 
# e.g. ~/.aliases -> ./aliases
# ~/.config/zed -> ./config/zed
files=(
  aliases
  aliases.local
  config/ghostty
  config/karabiner
  config/kitty
  config/nvim
  config/opencode
  config/codex
  config/claude
  config/cursor
  config/presenterm
  config/raycast
  config/zed
  eslintrc
  gitconfig
  gitconfig.local
  gitignore
  gitmessage
  ohmyzshrc
  pnpm-completion.zsh
  profile
  tool-versions
  warp
  zshrc
  zshrc.local
)
```

Lets rewrite all of this logic in lua. Each of the files I have listed there should be in the associative array with the value as the key and the `.[val]` as the value
I need to call this and wait for it to finish before continuing: `sh ./vim-setup.sh`
does os.execute pause execution unil it returns or is it async?

great, finally I need to do these:
```
# make a symlink to this directory for self-reference
ln -sfn "$dir" ~/.dotdir

# add scratch directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p ~/Documents/scratch

# Add a symlink to my scratch file that syncs through icloud drive
ln -s ~/Documents/scratch ~/git/scratch
```
how can I run my lua script from the command line
Remove any work having to do with google/federated for now. We don't have it and will add it later.
I don't understand this point: On sign-up confirmation, do not store passwords for auto-login; redirect user to sign in.
ok, we don't want to do that. When they sign up they shouldn't need to sign-in again, we may need the password for a short time.
after these changes I don't think we should need MOCK_ACCESS_TOKEN or VITE_TEMP_BYPASS_AUTH, can you explain what the work to do with those is?
ok, great, please update the plan with that work
why do we need SESSION_SECRET and VITE_AUTH_CLIENT_ID in org-next? We shouldn't be connecting to cognito or doing anything with cognito in org-next right?

what would the issues be if we had only one secret in the auth app for all applications that need to connect including org-next or future apps? This way we can avoid switch logic in auth to figure out which secret to use. What am I missing here?
What is the logic that would be required to have separate secrets for each app? Is this complex and across a systems or pretty simple in the auth app?

I just switched branches to an updated version of the auth app. Please run through the plan and look at the codebase making any updates based on the new code that would be required or other considerations having to do with the auth app as it is in code now.
If you were me, what other questions would you ask about this plan?
Great, please provide a brief answer to each of these questions and also ask me any followup questions you may have about the final plan

This is great, I want to deeply understand the changes, can you create a flow chart and a sequence diagram with descriptions to fully catch me up on how this auth system works and how it is meant to work with the org-next app
